message:
fix(test): repair 4 failing lib tests — detail scroll clamping + snapshot update (bd-vv5vh)

Three detail-scroll tests failed because DetailScrolled/PageScrolled handlers
clamp to max_scroll = detail_content_lines - detail_visible_height, both of
which default to 0 in CassApp::default(). Fixed by setting detail_content_lines
and detail_visible_height to realistic values in each test.

Blessed high-contrast snapshot to match pill editability indicator (✎ prefix)
added in 3e7c919.

Also includes input autocomplete infrastructure for filter pills.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1764","title":"Query Intelligence: Understanding and Improving Queries","description":"# Query Intelligence

## The Problem
Agents often struggle with queries:
1. **Why did this return 0 results?** - Was the query malformed? Too specific?
2. **How was my query interpreted?** - Did it understand my intent?
3. **What would work better?** - Suggestions for improvement

## The Solution
Make the query engine transparent and helpful:
- Explain how queries are parsed and executed
- Suggest alternatives when queries fail
- Allow dry-run validation

## Subtasks
1. **rob.query.explain** - Query explanation (--explain)
2. **rob.query.suggest** - Suggestions and did-you-mean
3. **rob.query.dry** - Dry-run mode (--dry-run)

## Value for Agents
- Self-correction: Agents can fix their own queries
- Learning: Understand query syntax through examples
- Confidence: Know when a query is reliable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.420280752Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.004523524Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:04:11.376139Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1m6m","title":"[EPIC] HTML Session Export: Beautiful, Encrypted, Cross-Platform","description":"# Epic: Premium HTML Session Export

## Overview
Create a world-class feature for exporting coding agent sessions as beautifully formatted, 
self-contained static HTML files. This feature provides both TUI and robot mode access,
with optional password-based encryption using Web Crypto API for client-side decryption.

## Strategic Goals
1. **Knowledge Preservation**: Allow users to archive and share valuable coding sessions
2. **Cross-Agent Value**: Export sessions from ANY agent (Claude, Codex, Cursor, etc.)
3. **Security-First**: Optional encryption ensures sensitive content stays protected
4. **Universal Access**: Files work offline, on any device, with or without internet
5. **Stripe-Level UX**: Premium visual design matching cass's TUI quality standards

## Key Features
- Self-contained HTML files with embedded CSS/JS
- Progressive enhancement: works offline, enhanced with CDN (Tailwind, Prism.js)
- Optional AES-256-GCM encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation
- Cross-platform download folder detection (Linux, macOS, Windows)
- Smart file naming: agent_project_datetime_topic.html
- Beautiful conversation rendering with syntax highlighting
- Dark/light mode toggle in exported file
- Print-friendly styles for PDF export from browser
- Search within exported conversation
- Collapsible tool calls and code blocks
- Full TUI modal with preview and options
- Complete robot mode API for automation

## Technical Approach
- Rust-side: HTML template generation with minified inline CSS/JS
- Encryption: PBKDF2 (WebCrypto-compatible) + AES-GCM with explicit salt/iv/iterations
- Web Crypto: PBKDF2 for key derivation (client-side compatible)
- File naming: Sanitize and slugify with chrono timestamps
- Downloads: dirs::download_dir() with fallback strategies

## Research Sources
- StatiCrypt: https://github.com/robinmoisson/staticrypt (static HTML encryption patterns)
- Web Crypto API: https://gist.github.com/chrisveness/43bcda93af9f646d083fad678071b90a
- Tailwind Chat UI: https://flowbite.com/docs/components/chat-bubble/
- Prism.js CDN: https://prismjs.com/ (syntax highlighting)
- Print CSS: https://print-css.rocks/ (print-friendly styles)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Export works for all 11 supported agents
- [ ] File opens correctly in Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge
- [ ] Encryption/decryption works in all browsers
- [ ] Offline mode displays conversation correctly
- [ ] Print to PDF produces readable output
- [ ] TUI modal is intuitive and polished
- [ ] Robot mode provides complete API coverage","notes":"### QA Gate (must satisfy before closing)

 {"id":"bd-1ueu","title":"Perf: lazy SQLite open to cut startup cost","description":"# Perf: Lazy SQLite Open to Cut Startup Cost

## Goal
Delay opening SQLite until a command actually needs it, reducing CLI/TUI startup time.

## Strategy
- Introduce a lazy initializer for DB pool/connection.
- Keep config parsing and help paths DB-free.
- Ensure thread-safety and single initialization.

## Logging
- Startup timing logs: pre-init, post-init, and reason for DB open.

## Tests
- Unit: lazy init only when DB functions are called; concurrent callers share one open.
- Integration: commands that should not touch DB (help/health) do not open it.
- E2E: startup benchmark script logs cold start times before/after.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Measurable startup reduction (record ms).
- No regressions in DB-required commands.
- Logs include open reason and duration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T19:20:35.430011738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T02:34:59.735628215Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T02:34:59.735384155Z","close_reason":"Implemented LazyDb struct in storage/sqlite.rs with parking_lot::Mutex<Option<Connection>>.  {"id":"bd-28iz","title":"P6.14b: No-mock policy + allowlist + CI gate","description":"# P6.14b: No-mock policy + allowlist + CI gate

## Goal
Define and enforce a strict no-mock policy for tests, with an explicit allowlist for unavoidable OS/hardware boundaries, and CI enforcement to prevent regressions.

## Why
Without a policy, mocks/fakes will creep back in. CI should fail fast on new mock/fake usage unless explicitly approved.

## Scope
- Policy definition (what is allowed, what is forbidden, how to request an exception).
- Allowlist format (file path + rationale + expiration date).
- CI gate: fail on new Mock/fake/stub usage outside allowlist.

## Steps
1. Draft policy section in `TESTING.md` (no-mock definition + rationale).
2. Create allowlist file (e.g., `test-results/no_mock_allowlist.json`) with structured entries.
3. Add a CI check (e.g., `scripts/validate_ci.sh`) that:
   - runs `rg`/`ast-grep` over `src/` + `tests/`
   - diff against allowlist
   - emits a report with violations
4. Provide a local opt-out for developers (explicit env var), but keep CI strict.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Policy section is explicit and discoverable.
- CI fails on new mock/fake usage without allowlist entry.
- Allowlist includes rationale + owner + review date.

## Dependencies
- Uses output from P6.14a audit to seed allowlist.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T06:00:30.928058148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T08:05:32.761866266Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T08:05:32.761662352Z","close_reason":"Completed: (1) TESTING.md created with no-mock policy, (2) scripts/validate_ci.sh CI gate working, (3) test-results/no_mock_allowlist.json with structured entries.  {"id":"bd-2dcc","title":"Dry-Run Mode (--dry-run)","description":"# Dry-Run Mode (--dry-run)

## Problem Statement
Agents want to validate queries without executing them:
- Is the syntax valid?
- How will it be interpreted?
- What would the cost be?

Currently, the only way to check is to run the query and examine results.

## Proposed Solution
Add `--dry-run` flag that validates and analyzes without executing:
```bash
cass search "complex query" --dry-run --json
```

Output:
```json
{
  "dry_run": true,
  "valid": true,
  "explanation": {...},
  "estimated_results": "10-100",
  "estimated_cost_ms": "<50",
  "warnings": []
}
```

## Design Decisions

### What Dry-Run Does
1. Parse the query (full validation)
2. Analyze filters and constraints

        messages = session.messages.len(),

    assert!(html.contains("\"ciphertext\":"));

    assert!(!logs_contain(password), "Password leaked to logs!");

 {"id":"bd-u4me","title":"Implement smart filename generation with cross-platform support","description":"# Task: Smart Filename Generation

## Context
Generate human-friendly, filesystem-safe filenames that encode useful metadata.
Handle cross-platform differences and provide automatic downloads folder detection.

## Filename Format
```
{agent}_{workspace}_{date}_{time}_{topic}.html
```

Example: `claude_code_myproject_2024_01_15_1430_fix_auth_bug.html`

## Components

### 1. Agent Slug (src/html_export/filename.rs)
```rust
fn agent_slug(agent: &str) -> String {
    // Normalize common variations
    match agent.to_lowercase().as_str() {
        "claude_code" | "claude-code" | "claudecode" => "claude",
        "cursor" => "cursor",
        "chatgpt" | "chat_gpt" => "chatgpt",
        "gemini" | "gemini-cli" => "gemini",
        "codex" => "codex",
        "aider" => "aider",
        "pi_agent" | "pi-agent" => "piagent",
        "factory" | "droid" => "factory",
        "opencode" => "opencode",
        "cline" => "cline",
        "amp" => "amp",
        other => slugify(other, 15),
    }
}
```

### 2. Workspace Component
```rust
fn workspace_slug(workspace: Option<&Path>) -> String {
    match workspace {
        Some(path) => {
            // Get last component (project name)
            let name = path.file_name()
                .and_then(|n| n.to_str())
                .unwrap_or("unknown");
            slugify(name, 20)
        }
        None => "standalone".to_string()
    }
}
```

### 3. DateTime Component
```rust
fn datetime_slug(timestamp: Option<i64>) -> String {
    let dt = timestamp
        .and_then(|ts| DateTime::from_timestamp_millis(ts))
        .unwrap_or_else(Utc::now);
    
    dt.format("%Y_%m_%d_%H%M").to_string()
    // Output: 2024_01_15_1430
}
```

### 4. Topic Extraction
```rust
fn topic_slug(conversation: &Conversation) -> String {
    // Priority order:
    // 1. Explicit title
    // 2. First user message (truncated)
    // 3. First message of any kind
    // 4. Fallback "session"
    
    let raw = conversation.title.as_deref()
        .or_else(|| conversation.messages.iter()
            .find(|m| matches!(m.role, MessageRole::User))
            .map(|m| m.content.as_str()))
        .or_else(|| conversation.messages.first()
            .map(|m| m.content.as_str()))
        .unwrap_or("session");
    
    // Extract meaningful words, skip code/urls
    let words: Vec<&str> = raw.split_whitespace()
        .filter(|w| !w.starts_with("http"))
        .filter(|w| !w.contains('/'))
        .filter(|w| w.len() < 20)
        .take(5)
        .collect();
    
    let topic = words.join("_");
    slugify(&topic, 30)
}
```

### 5. Slugify Function
```rust
fn slugify(input: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {
    let slug: String = input
        .to_lowercase()
        .chars()
        .map(|c| match c {
            'a'..='z' | '0'..='9' => c,
            ' ' | '-' | '_' => '_',
            _ => '_',
        })
        .collect();
    
    // Collapse multiple underscores
    let collapsed: String = slug
        .split('_')
        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
        .join("_");
    
    // Truncate to max length
    if collapsed.len() <= max_len {
        collapsed
    } else {
        let truncated: String = collapsed.chars().take(max_len).collect();
        truncated.trim_end_matches('_').to_string()
    }
}
```

### 6. Full Filename Generation
```rust
pub fn generate_filename(conversation: &Conversation) -> String {
    let agent = agent_slug(&conversation.agent_slug);
    let workspace = workspace_slug(conversation.workspace.as_deref());
    let datetime = datetime_slug(conversation.started_at);
    let topic = topic_slug(conversation);
    
    format!("{agent}_{workspace}_{datetime}_{topic}.html")
}
```

## Cross-Platform Downloads Folder

### Using dirs crate (already in Cargo.toml)
```rust
pub fn get_downloads_dir() -> PathBuf {
    // Primary: Platform-specific downloads
    if let Some(downloads) = dirs::download_dir() {
        return downloads;
    }
    
    // Fallback 1: Home + Downloads
    if let Some(home) = dirs::home_dir() {
        let fallback = home.join("Downloads");
        if fallback.exists() {
            return fallback;
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback 2: Current directory
    std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from("."))
}
```

### Platform Behaviors
| Platform | dirs::download_dir() | Fallback |
|----------|---------------------|----------|
| Linux | $XDG_DOWNLOAD_DIR or ~/Downloads | ~/Downloads |
| macOS | ~/Downloads | ~/Downloads |
| Windows | Known Folder: Downloads | %USERPROFILE%\Downloads |

### Filename Collision Handling
```rust
pub fn unique_filename(dir: &Path, base: &str) -> PathBuf {
    let path = dir.join(base);
    if !path.exists() {
        return path;
    }
    
    // Add numeric suffix: file_1.html, file_2.html, ...
    let stem = Path::new(base).file_stem()
        .and_then(|s| s.to_str())
        .unwrap_or(base);
    
    for i in 1..1000 {
        let new_name = format!("{}_{}.html", stem, i);
        let new_path = dir.join(&new_name);
        if !new_path.exists() {
            return new_path;
        }
    }
    
    // Ultimate fallback: UUID
    dir.join(format!("{}_{}.html", stem, uuid::Uuid::new_v4()))
}
```

 {"id":"bd-w9z0","title":"Design HTML template architecture and file structure","description":"# Task: Design HTML Template Architecture

## Context
We need a robust template system that generates self-contained HTML files for session export.
This is the foundational architecture that all other components build upon.

## Deliverables

### 1. Template Module Structure
Create `src/html_export/` module with:
```
src/html_export/
├── mod.rs           # Module facade, re-exports
├── template.rs      # Core HTML template generation
├── styles.rs        # CSS (inline + Tailwind CDN fallback)
├── scripts.rs       # JS (decryption, search, theme toggle)
├── renderer.rs      # Conversation -> HTML rendering
├── filename.rs      # Smart filename generation
└── encryption.rs    # Web Crypto compatible encryption
```

### 2. Template Architecture
Design a template system that:
- Uses string interpolation (not external template engine) for simplicity
- Inlines critical CSS for offline operation
- Lazy-loads CDN resources for enhanced experience
- Separates concerns: structure, styling, behavior

### 3. HTML Document Structure
```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en" data-theme="dark">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
    <title>{title}</title>
    <!-- Critical inline styles for offline -->
    <style>{critical_css}</style>
    <!-- CDN enhancement (Tailwind) -->
    <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com" defer></script>
    <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/prismjs@1/themes/prism-tomorrow.min.css">
    <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/prismjs@1/prism.min.js" defer></script>
    <!-- Print styles -->
    <style media="print">{print_css}</style>
</head>
<body>
    <div id="app">
        <!-- Header with metadata -->
        <!-- Toolbar: search, theme toggle, print -->
        <!-- Conversation container -->
        <!-- Password modal (if encrypted) -->
    </div>
    <!-- Scripts at end for performance -->
    <script>{inline_js}</script>
</body>
</html>
```

### 4. Design Decisions
- **No template engine dependency**: Use Rust format! macros and string building
- **Inline everything critical**: Ensures offline functionality
- **CDN as enhancement**: Tailwind, Prism.js loaded with defer
- **Progressive enhancement**: Basic layout works without JS
- **Semantic HTML**: Proper use of article, section, header elements

### 5. Color Palette Mapping
Match TUI theme.rs colors for consistency:
- BG_DEEP: #1a1b26 -> CSS variable --bg-deep
- BG_SURFACE: #24283b -> --bg-surface
- TEXT_PRIMARY: #c0caf5 -> --text-primary
- ACCENT_PRIMARY: #7aa2f7 -> --accent
- ROLE_USER: #9ece6a -> --role-user
- ROLE_AGENT: #7aa2f7 -> --role-agent
- ROLE_TOOL: #ff9e64 -> --role-tool
- ROLE_SYSTEM: #e0af68 -> --role-system

- [ ] Template generates valid HTML5
- [ ] Critical CSS enables readable offline view
- [ ] CDN resources enhance (not break) offline view
- [ ] Color scheme matches TUI aesthetics
- [ ] Document structure follows accessibility best practices","notes":"### Testing & Logging

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-3rd0b","title":"Add background color to search input area for visual separation","description":"## What

Add a subtle inset background to the search query input row, making it visually distinguishable from the surrounding search bar block. Currently the input area inherits the parent pane background, making the text field invisible as a distinct UI element.

## Where

- **app.rs:~11738-11747** — Query text rendering:
  ```rust
  let query_row = rows[0];
  let query_style = if self.input_mode == InputMode::Query && self.query.is_empty() {
      text_muted_style
  } else {
      styles.style(style_system::STYLE_TEXT_PRIMARY)
  };
  Paragraph::new(elide_text(&query_text, query_row.width as usize))
      .style(query_style)
      .render(query_row, frame);
  ```

## Target
Add a block with inset background BEFORE the query text:
```rust
// Inset background for text field affordance
let input_bg = if apply_style {
    Style::new().bg(to_packed(blend(resolved.surface, resolved.background, 0.5)))
} else { plain };
Block::new().style(input_bg).render(query_row, frame);
// Then render query text on top
Paragraph::new(elide_text(&query_text, ...))
    .style(query_style)
    .render(query_row, frame);
```

## Visual Effect
The input row becomes slightly darker/lighter than the surrounding block, creating a "text field" look similar to web input elements. The effect is SUBTLE — a 50% blend between surface and background, not a jarring color change.

## New Style Token (Optional)
Consider defining `STYLE_INPUT_BG` in style_system.rs:
```rust
pub const STYLE_INPUT_BG: &str = "input.bg";
sheet.define(STYLE_INPUT_BG, Style::new()
    .bg(to_packed(blend(resolved.surface, resolved.background, 0.5))));
```
This would make the input field background theme-aware and customizable.

## Required Tests

### Unit tests:
1. `test_search_input_has_background` — Render the search bar, verify the query row has a non-default background style applied (bg is Some).
2. `test_search_input_bg_differs_from_pane` — Verify the input bg color differs from STYLE_PANE_BASE bg.
3. `test_search_input_placeholder_visible` — With empty query, verify placeholder text ("<type to search>") is visible against the input background (contrast check).
4. `test_search_input_degradation` — At NoStyling, verify input bg drops to plain.

### Snapshot test:
5. Update `cassapp_results_wide.snap` to show the input area with visible background distinction.

## Considerations
- The blend ratio (0.5) may need tuning. Too strong → jarring; too subtle → invisible. Start with 0.5, test across all 6 presets, adjust if needed.
- On Light theme, the inset should be DARKER. On Dark theme, it should be LIGHTER (or slightly different shade of dark). The blend function handles this naturally since it interpolates between two theme colors.
- The pill row (row 2) and breadcrumb row (row 3) do NOT get the inset — only the query input row.
- This is a lower-impact visual change but contributes to the overall "designed" feel.

## Acceptance
- Query input row has visible background different from surrounding pane
- Placeholder text readable against background
- All 6 presets produce appropriate inset effect
- Degradation strips background at NoStyling+
- 4 unit tests + snapshot update pass
